org.jlab.epsci.ersap.std.services

Class AbstractEventWriterService<Writer>

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.jlab.epsci.ersap.std.services.AbstractService
      • org.jlab.epsci.ersap.std.services.AbstractEventWriterService<Writer>
  • Type Parameters:
    Writer - the class for the user-defined writer of the given data-type
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    Engine

    public abstract class AbstractEventWriterService<Writer>
extends AbstractService
    An abstract writer service that writes all received events into the configured output file.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      protected Writer writer
      The writer object.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      AbstractEventWriterService() 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      protected abstract void closeWriter()
      Closes the writer and its output file.
      EngineData configure(EngineData input)
      Configures the engine with the given input data.
      protected abstract Writer createWriter(java.nio.file.Path file, org.json.JSONObject opts)
      Creates a new writer and opens the given output file.
      void destroy()
      Destroy this engine, closing and cleaning up any opened resources.
      EngineData execute(EngineData input)
      Executes the engine with the given input data.
      EngineData executeGroup(java.util.Set<EngineData> inputs)
      Executes the engine with the given set of input data.
      protected abstract EngineDataType getDataType()
      Gets the ERSAP engine data-type for the type of the events.
      java.util.Set<EngineDataType> getInputDataTypes()
      Gets the set of input data types supported by the engine.
      java.util.Set<EngineDataType> getOutputDataTypes()
      Gets the set of output data types supported by the engine.
      void reset()
      Resets this engine to its initial setup.
      protected abstract void writeEvent(java.lang.Object event)
      Writes an event to the output file.

        getAuthor, getDescription, getName, getStates, getVersion

    • Field Detail

      • writer

        protected Writer writer
        The writer object.

    • Constructor Detail

      • AbstractEventWriterService

        public AbstractEventWriterService()

    • Method Detail

      • configure

        public EngineData configure(EngineData input)
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Configures the engine with the given input data.

        This method can be executed concurrently in several threads.

        Parameters:
        input - the data to configure the engine with
        Returns:
        an (optional) result and/or status of the configuration request, it could be null or without output data (but not recommended)

      • createWriter

        protected abstract Writer createWriter(java.nio.file.Path file,
                                       org.json.JSONObject opts)
                                throws EventWriterException
        Creates a new writer and opens the given output file.
        Parameters:
        file - the path to the output file
        opts - extra options for the writer
        Returns:
        a new writer ready to writer events to the output file
        Throws:
        EventWriterException - if the writer could not be created

      • closeWriter

        protected abstract void closeWriter()
        Closes the writer and its output file.

      • execute

        public EngineData execute(EngineData input)
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Executes the engine with the given input data.

        This method can be executed concurrently in several threads.

        Parameters:
        input - the data to execute the engine with
        Returns:
        the result and/or status of the execution request, it cannot be null or without data (unless it is an error status), and it should not be the same input data reference

      • executeGroup

        public EngineData executeGroup(java.util.Set<EngineData> inputs)
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Executes the engine with the given set of input data.

        This method can be executed concurrently in several threads.

        Specified by:
        executeGroup in interface Engine
        Overrides:
        executeGroup in class AbstractService
        Parameters:
        inputs - the data set to execute the engine with
        Returns:
        the result and/or status of the execution request, it cannot be null or without data (unless it is an error status), and it should not be the same as one of the input data references

      • writeEvent

        protected abstract void writeEvent(java.lang.Object event)
                            throws EventWriterException
        Writes an event to the output file. The event should be a Java object with the same type as the one defined by the ERSAP engine data-type returned by getDataType().
        Parameters:
        event - the event to be written
        Throws:
        EventWriterException - if the file could not be read

      • getDataType

        protected abstract EngineDataType getDataType()
        Gets the ERSAP engine data-type for the type of the events. The data-type will be used to deserialize the events when the engine data is received from services across the network.
        Returns:
        the data-type of the events

      • getInputDataTypes

        public java.util.Set<EngineDataType> getInputDataTypes()
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Gets the set of input data types supported by the engine.
        Returns:
        the allowed input data types, it cannot be null nor empty

      • getOutputDataTypes

        public java.util.Set<EngineDataType> getOutputDataTypes()
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Gets the set of output data types supported by the engine.
        Returns:
        the allowed output data types, it cannot be null nor empty

      • reset

        public void reset()
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Resets this engine to its initial setup. It should at least revert any changes done by configuration requests and side-effects of execute requests.

      • destroy

        public void destroy()
        Description copied from interface: Engine
        Destroy this engine, closing and cleaning up any opened resources.
